mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes do not predict negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns with Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 235.0, persistent inflation pressures force central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising unemployment indicates a decoupling of output and labor demand, signaling recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 105.0, severe manufacturing contraction triggers immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in >50% of triggered instances or directional accuracy falls below 53% in walk-forward tests.
